sections.paragraph:
ὑποδεής, ές (δέομαι)

① pert. to being in a lower position, inferior in gener. lit. (Hdt. et al., likew. IG IV2/1, 91, 3 [III a.d.]; pap, Joseph.) always in the comp. ὑποδεέστερος, α, ον, pl. οἱ ὑποδεέστεροι ‘those who are inferior’; so also Dg 10:5.

② pert. to being responsive to authority, subservient in a transf. sense as sing. subst. τὸ ὑποδεές τινος someone’s subordination (w. τὸ ταπεινόφρον) 1 Cl 19:1.—DELG s.v. ὑπό.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

gener. gener. = generally

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

likew. likew. = likewise

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

  1. pl. = plural

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

transf. transf. = transferred

sing. sing. = singular

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1036
